Class Widget

java.lang.Object
io.github.isoyigido.basic.gui.core.Widget
public final class Widget extends Object
Represents a displayable widget that contains a Component object. Forwards the rendering, update, and input event methods to the contained Component object. Holds the location of the contained Component object on the screen, and handles local coordinate transformations for rendering and input event methods.
See Also:
  • Component
  • GUI

    Represents anchor points on a widget.

    Returns the layer index of this widget, which dictates which widget is rendered on top and receives the input events first.

  • Constructor Details

    • Widget

      public Widget(Component component, int anchorX, int anchorY, Widget.Anchor anchor)
      Constructs a widget that contains the given component and is anchored to the given point on the screen.
      Parameters:
      component - the component that is contained in the widget
      anchorX - the x-coordinate of the anchor point on the screen
      anchorY - the y-coordinate of the anchor point on the screen
      anchor - the point on the widget anchored to the anchor point
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the input component is null

  • Method Details

    • render

      public void render(Graphics2D g)
      Renders the component contained in this widget. Forwards the render call to each child widget.
      Parameters:
      g - the graphics context to render on

    • update

      public void update()
      Updates the component contained in this widget. Forwards the update call to each child widget.

      Returns the component contained in this widget.

      Returns the y-coordinate of the top-left corner of this widget.
      Returns:

      public int getLayerIndex()
      Returns the layer index of this widget, which dictates which widget is rendered on top and receives the input events first.
      Returns:

      this

      Parameters:
